stories.title: CPSC Warning — Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Immediately [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
🚨 CPSC warning: Stop using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets now.

Strangulation + fall hazard. ~250 units sold on Amazon 2025. Company REFUSED to recall — parents must: remove padding, cut cover in half, discard both.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
🚨 If you have a Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinet, the CPSC wants you to stop using it immediately.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission issued an urgent warning this week about these bassinets, which were sold on Amazon between April and October 2025 for about $40. The problem: the restraints inside pose a strangulation hazard to infants, and the sides are too low to contain a baby, creating a fall risk. There's no stand either, which means a serious fall risk if placed on an elevated surface.

Here's where it gets complicated: the manufacturer, Tuyedoqe (based in China), was issued a Notice of Violation by the CPSC — but they've refused to issue a recall or offer any remedy to consumers. That means there's no refund, no exchange, and no company coming to fix this.

What you should do right now: stop use immediately, remove the foam sleeping pad, cut the cover in half, and dispose of both. The bassinets came in pink, green, gray, sky blue, and wine red.

If you bought this on Amazon, check your order history — the sale window was April through October 2025.

Have you seen this product, or do you know other parents who might have it? Share this post so the word gets out.

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: CPSC safety warning: Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets pose strangulation and fall hazards. Company refused to recall. Stop use now and dispose of the product.

article.headline: CPSC Warning: Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Immediately

stories.title: D49 and D20 Lead Colorado in New Purple Star School Designations [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
COS schools lead Colorado in military family support.

D49 earned the MOST Purple Star School designations in the state. D20 earned second most. 21 of 22 new designations went to Southern Colorado schools.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
This is a big deal for Colorado Springs military families.

On April 7, Governor Polis and the Colorado Department of Education recognized 22 new Purple Star Schools across the state — schools that provide dedicated support for military-connected kids. Of those 22, a remarkable 21 are in Southern Colorado.

And COS led the way: D49 earned the most Purple Star school designations of any district in Colorado. D20 came in second.

The Purple Star designation is meaningful. It's not just an honor — it's a commitment. Schools have to maintain a dedicated military liaison, keep resources for military families on their website, and run a student-led program to help military kids transition smoothly. Given that Colorado Springs is home to Fort Carson, NORAD, Peterson Space Force Base, and Schriever Space Force Base, these programs serve a real and essential need.

Military families move frequently. Kids change schools. Having schools that are prepared, resourced, and welcoming makes a genuine difference.

If your family is military and you're wondering which schools earned the Purple Star designation, check with D49 or D20 directly — and watch for announcements from individual schools.

Does your family have a connection to the military? We'd love to hear your experience with Colorado Springs schools.

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: D49 leads Colorado, D20 ranks second: 21 of 22 new Purple Star Schools are in Southern Colorado. A big win for COS military families.

article.headline: D49 Leads Colorado, D20 Ranks Second in New Purple Star School Designations for Military Families

stories.title: 3rd Annual Touch-a-Truck FREE Event at Norris Penrose — May 16 [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
FREE family event: 3rd Annual Touch-a-Truck, May 16.

9:30am–2pm at Norris Penrose. Trucks, military vehicles, helicopters. New timed entry this year. Free tickets required: norrispenrose.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
One of Colorado Springs' best free family events is back — and this year, they're expecting up to 15,000 people.

The 3rd Annual Touch-a-Truck takes place on Saturday, May 16 from 9:30 a.m. to 2 p.m. at Norris Penrose Event Center (1045 Lower Gold Camp Road). It's completely free, and kids of all ages can get up close with first-responder vehicles, military equipment, sanitation trucks, construction machinery, community service vehicles, and even helicopters (weather permitting).

New this year: timed entry. Organizers added it to ease parking congestion and cut down on wait times — which means a much smoother experience than in previous years. You'll need to claim your free timed-entry tickets in advance at norrispenrose.com.

The 2025 event drew over 11,000 people. Mark May 16 on the calendar now, and grab your tickets before they fill up.

Is this event on your family's to-do list? What vehicles are your kids most excited about?

---

#### Image Caption -- 12:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: FREE Touch-a-Truck event: May 16, 9:30am–2pm at Norris Penrose. Timed entry is new this year — free tickets at norrispenrose.com.

article.headline: 3rd Annual Touch-a-Truck Returns May 16 — Free Event at Norris Penrose

stories.title: D49 Families — Last Day and Graduation Dates Are Set [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
D49 families — mark your calendars:

May 15: PPEC/SSAE/Patriot graduation at COS City Hub
May 20: Last day for preschool
May 22: Last day for students (3-hr early release)
May 23: FHS/SCHS/VRHS graduation at World Arena

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families, the end of the school year is closer than it feels — and there's a lot happening in the final stretch.

Here are the key dates to add to your calendar now:

Friday, May 15 — Graduation ceremonies for PPEC, SSAE, and Patriot High School, held at COS City Hub.

Wednesday, May 20 — Last day for preschool students.

Friday, May 22 — Last day for students in grades K-12. This is a 3-hour early release day for everyone, so plan your childcare accordingly.

Saturday, May 23 — Graduation ceremonies for Falcon High School, Sand Creek High School, and Vista Ridge High School, all at World Arena.

Monday, May 25 — Memorial Day. District closed.

Thursday, May 28 — Last day for teachers.

The World Arena graduations are a big moment — if you've got a senior at FHS, SCHS, or VRHS, May 23 is the day.

Are you counting down the days? What does your family have planned for the first week of summer?

---

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: D49 end-of-year reminder: May 22 last day (3-hr early release) | May 23 World Arena graduations | May 15 City Hub graduations. Summer's almost here.

article.headline: D49 Families: Here Are Your End-of-Year and Graduation Dates for 2026

stories.title: Pediatricians Are Now Asking About Guns at Well-Child Checkups [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Your child's pediatrician may ask about guns at the next well-child checkup.

It's an AAP recommendation — new 2026 research from the PAS conference shows more providers are now screening for firearm storage at home.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your child's pediatrician asks about guns at the next well-child checkup, don't be surprised — it's part of a growing evidence-based approach to keeping kids safe.

New research presented at the 2026 Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) meeting in Boston showed that quality improvement programs can meaningfully increase the rate at which doctors screen for firearm storage in the home during well-child visits. The American Academy of Pediatrics has long recommended that pediatricians ask parents about whether firearms are present in the home and whether they're stored safely.

For Colorado Springs families — with one of the largest military populations in the country — this conversation is especially relevant. Legal gun ownership is common in our community, and safe storage is the single most effective way to prevent accidental shootings and youth suicide.

Safe storage doesn't mean giving up your firearm. It means: locked gun safes, trigger locks, ammunition stored separately, firearms stored unloaded. These measures are proven to reduce the risk of a child accessing a gun.

If your child's doctor brings it up at the next checkup, take it as the health and safety conversation it's intended to be. And if you'd like more information on free or low-cost gun lock programs in Colorado Springs, resources are available through local community health organizations.

Does your family talk about safe storage? How do you handle the gun safety conversation with your kids?

---

#### Image Caption -- 5:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: Pediatricians are adding firearm safety to well-child checkups — an AAP recommendation that's especially relevant for Colorado Springs' military-connected families.

article.headline: Pediatricians Are Now Screening for Firearm Safety at Well-Child Visits — Here's What COS Parents Need to Know
